info.title: Geohash.Polyhasher.CLI
info.description:
Converts geometries such as polygons and linestrings to optimized sets of geohashes.

        This is the NuGet/dotnet command line tool which will let you encode geometries as sets of geohashes on the command line.

options.name: --format
options.description:
(Default: WKT) Input format. Supported formats are:
WKT. Default value: WKT

options.name: --precision
options.description:
(Default: 6) The geohash precision level to use when
hashing the geometries. Default value: 6

options.name: --mode
options.description:
(Default: Intersects) Which mode to use. Supported
modes are: Intersect, Contains. Default: Intersect

options.name: --edge-handling
options.description:
(Default: WGS84) How geometry edges are interpreted.
Supported values: WGS84, WebMercator, Geodesic.
Default: WGS84

options.name: --output-geometry
options.description:
(Default: false) Enable this to output the result as
CSV with WKB geometries added for individual hashes.
Default value: false

options.name: --headers
options.description:
(Default: false) If output-geometry is enabled, then
enable this to add CSV headers to the output. Default
value: false

options.name: --separator
options.description:
(Default: ;) If output-geometry is enabled, this
option can be used to customize the column separator
